post.raw_content:
I'm so sick of straight people turning every #game with #LGBT people in it into a political thing.

For real people, it's games. Leave the politics out of it.

replies.raw_content:
I... was not expecting so many people to miss the point

Why am I like this
@EvilSqueegee This is hilarious. Thank you. I'm sorry that it's flying over so many heads.

nested_replies.raw_content: @ChrisDelvo Shit happens, lol. Glad I brightened your day.

replies.raw_content: @EvilSqueegee If you're going the leave politics out of it route because it's a game; why does sexual orientation of a character matter to a game? If you didn't know would it really impact the story? Even if they do tell you, does it still really matter to the whole story?

nested_replies.raw_content: @GrindyMcGrindy Exactly. So why not share it? Leaving it out is just political BS.

@EvilSqueegee But it's not really political if the characters orientation doesn't matter to the game. It's just pandering if there's nothing to the story to their orientation and should've been left out to fix a plot hole.
@GrindyMcGrindy The demand that gayness serve the plot is political af. Why should it?

@EvilSqueegee Because if you're going to go out of the way to be like hey I'm gay, it better serve the story being told. If it serves no purpose why do you care? It's literally pandering and if it doesn't develop into something that's shallow and doesnt help character development if no purpose

@GrindyMcGrindy This makes literally no sense.

Characters have alignments, eye colors, hobbies, phobias, and surnames all of which frequently mean nothing to the plot

BUT SEXUALITY? TOO FAR MAN. TOO FAR.

That is literally a political agenda. 😂

@EvilSqueegee But alignments do matter as to how the character plays. If they break character thats plot breaking for the story.Eye color and everything else is to describe the character as tehy are in the story. That literally matters to the story.

@GrindyMcGrindy "Sexuality has no impact on how people act"

-- this argument

@EvilSqueegee Developing characters isn't important to the story is your entire argument. Don't give them a modus operandi. You're also stereotyping that people act a certain way by their sexual prefence.

@GrindyMcGrindy That is literally not what any of my words mean, but sure, you can just decide that my point is the opposite of my point and also assume that I said sexuality has *certain* impacts on character's actions rather than just having an impact on their actions

I won't stop you lol.

@GrindyMcGrindy My point was that orientation and gender are a part of a character and therefore are a part of development as much as literally anything else, so labeling their orientation SPECIFICALLY as unimportant *is a political agenda* that has no place in a game, at the table, etc.
@GrindyMcGrindy And that a claim that orientation MUST SERVE THE PLOT is just politics being shoved down my throat, not actually a valid or justifiable stance on anything

@EvilSqueegee @GrindyMcGrindy You know what I like? In the Kingdom Hearts 3 trailer, Goofy remarks that the Pirates world is one Sora's always liked. Before that point we never really stopped to consider what worlds he likes and dislikes. It's characterization that has no bearing on the plot.

@Pookajutsu @EvilSqueegee In KH2 Sora said he always wanted to go on a pirate adventure, or wanted to be a pirate. Something along those lines. So we knew back in KH2 that Pirates was a favorite world of his. It was more about him living out a childhood dream which isn't necessarily a characterization.
@GrindyMcGrindy @Pookajutsu ...what?
@EvilSqueegee @Pookajutsu Does fulfilling a childhood dream define you as a character? Not necessarily. Sora as a kid wanting to be a pirate doesn't necessarily define Sora's character.

@GrindyMcGrindy @Pookajutsu But characterization is not required to encompass the entirety of your character.

Why would you even think that it did?
@GrindyMcGrindy @EvilSqueegee Fulfilling the dream doesn't. HAVING the dream does.

@GrindyMcGrindy @Pookajutsu @EvilSqueegee I feel like if the knowledge of a characters sexual orientation doesn't come out of left field, like the writing building up to hey this character is definitely 'this said thing' then it's good to know. But if they just shoehorn it in without actually building on it, then no.
@AquasSacrifice @GrindyMcGrindy @Pookajutsu Yeah I feel the same way about characters' musical taste, favorite foods, and eye color.
@EvilSqueegee @GrindyMcGrindy @Pookajutsu Eye color is okay to not even reference unless it's used to identify or theres something behind having certain eye colors

@AquasSacrifice @GrindyMcGrindy @Pookajutsu I was being sarcastic, but sure -- we can have a conversation about literally all description of characters that does not somehow create events in the story is just deadweight that needs to be cut.

I disagree with that idea. :P
@EvilSqueegee @GrindyMcGrindy @Pookajutsu Relavent.

@EvilSqueegee @GrindyMcGrindy @Pookajutsu Okay so like yeah, if they're are character profiles in the game, yeah sure throw it in there, what grindy was saying, why mention it in dialogue if it theres no reason to. Instead of just plainly saying it and mentioning it build it into the character. Talk about it when it is
@AquasSacrifice @EvilSqueegee @GrindyMcGrindy There's an interesting tangent to be discussed at some point in the future about mentions being more prevalent because straight is the "assumed norm."

@Pookajutsu @EvilSqueegee @GrindyMcGrindy Yeah I'll agree "assumed norm" is indeed straight. Which is why I said put it into his/her character in the way they act. Make it obvious the character the character isnt straight. Make a love interest for the character that you can pursue.

@AquasSacrifice @Pookajutsu @GrindyMcGrindy My problem here is threefold: 

1) Isn't it a bit off to imply all characters of a particular gender/orientation should act the same way

2) Isn't a character out-right stating their orientation/gender "making it obvious?"

3) Must all nonstraight characters have romance plots?

@EvilSqueegee @Pookajutsu @GrindyMcGrindy 3. No, you dont, even straight characters dont need it if it's not pertinent to your characters story or characterization. I hold the same standard for all orientations.

@AquasSacrifice @Pookajutsu @GrindyMcGrindy I'm confused. What's the difference between saying that gay characters should have love interests (if they do, cool, by why imply it's needed) and then saying they don't need romance plots?

Like, I'm bi even when I'm single and not looking for a relationship. It's not required.
@EvilSqueegee @Pookajutsu @GrindyMcGrindy Doesnt have to define my character, if I'm told through some 2nd hand way. Like said character filling out a form, or if character is asked.

@EvilSqueegee @Pookajutsu @GrindyMcGrindy There is no difference, I'm not saying that there has to be a love interest. I'm plainly saying that no matter what my characters orientation is, if I am plainly told, outside of generic anime character introduction, my characters orientation, I want it to be relavent.

@EvilSqueegee @Pookajutsu @GrindyMcGrindy 1 you're definitely correct on. Not everyone acts the same. 

2. While yes you're right. But do you start off conversations saying "Hi I'm staight, gay, bi, etc."? What's the reasoning you're saying that. Doesnt have to be a sub plot. I just want a natural reason.

@AquasSacrifice @Pookajutsu @GrindyMcGrindy I introduce myself with my pronouns so I'm not mis-gendered, as do MANY people. This is why people putting pronouns in their bio on twitter is a thing.

People being gay is already natural. There's literally nothing unnatural about a character being gay, or indicating it.
@Pookajutsu @EvilSqueegee @GrindyMcGrindy Too much "plot" not enough plot

@Pookajutsu @EvilSqueegee @GrindyMcGrindy It's the same for straight characters, i personally want good writing behind my characters personalities and preferences. I dont want game companies to make certain preferences to just appeal to certain people. Same reason why most ecchi anime sucks.
@EvilSqueegee @GrindyMcGrindy Now I'm being informed that's pandering to the people that voted Pirates over Ninjas in all those old early internet polls. Damnit.
